description: From the CincinnaliGazette LAW OF PERSONAL FREEDOM If the considerations we have adduced upon this subject needed any additional confirmation it would he found perhaps in the statues which have been passed in many of the Northern Stacs from courtesy to the South securing the slaveholder the right to his slave while traveling through or for a limited period sojourning in the State The very necessity of legislation implies that without it the right would not exist But such confirmation is hardly needed and for this we have only to re fer to the decisions of the Southern Courts themselves upon this subject It is a stri king instance of the consistency and beau ty and harmony of the law that while the English Courts above the tige in which they lived as Mr Justice Best declared themlie is not an Irishmanno Mr Compositornor has I believe a particle of the Irish in his veins laid down in the case of Sommersett the great principles of the law of personal freedom which should govern them the Courts of Louisiana and others of the slaveholding Stales at a re mote point both in space and time above and beyond the considerations
